verdict_evidence: Searched ci.yml (lines 31-244) and the pre-commit stack: no step regenerates docs/openapi.json and runs `git diff --exit-code`. The backend job (lines 32-82) covers ruff/mypy/vulture/radon/interrogate/codespell/pytest/pip-audit/consistency_lint, none of which import bearings.web.app.create_app to re-render the spec. consistency_lint.py is the closest analog but only checks route-fn naming and SQL column ordering (lines 22, 69), not OpenAPI drift. The CHANGELOG §1.0.0 stability promise treats `docs/openapi.json` as the SemVer surface (CHANGELOG.md:594), so an undetected drift can ship a breaking change unannounced.
acceptance_criteria: A new CI step (or pre-commit hook + CI invocation) regenerates docs/openapi.json by running `bearings.web.app.create_app().openapi()` and writes it to a tempfile.

The step then runs `git diff --exit-code -- docs/openapi.json` (or compares the parsed JSON to the committed file with `==`); non-zero exit fails CI with a message instructing the contributor to commit the regenerated file.
Step is gated to run on every push to v1-rebuild and every PR (matches existing backend job triggers).
executor_brief: Add an OpenAPI spec-drift gate to CI. Either author scripts/regen_openapi.py (dump create_app().openapi() to docs/openapi.json with the same indent=2 + trailing newline as CLAUDE.md prescribes) and add a `name: openapi spec drift` step to ci.yml's backend job that runs the script then `git diff --exit-code -- docs/openapi.json`, or wire the same as a pre-commit hook plus CI invocation. Goal: any PR that changes route surfaces without regenerating docs/openapi.json fails.

audit_text: CI: DB schema drift check (same pattern against `db/schema.sql`).
verdict_evidence: ci.yml has no step that materialises the live SQLite schema (e.g., open an in-memory aiosqlite, apply schema.sql, dump `sqlite_schema`) and diffs it against the committed schema.sql. consistency_lint.py references SCHEMA_REL_PATH at line 69 only to check column ordering — it does not regenerate or diff. The CHANGELOG §1.0.0 stability commitment includes the on-disk schema (CHANGELOG.md:613-616), so a runtime-vs-checked-in schema drift can ship undetected.
acceptance_criteria: A new script (e.g. scripts/check_schema_drift.py) opens a fresh aiosqlite memory DB, applies src/bearings/db/schema.sql, dumps the resulting schema (e.g. via `SELECT sql FROM sqlite_schema ORDER BY name`), and compares it to a deterministically-rendered version of the committed schema.sql.
A new ci.yml step `name: schema.sql drift` runs the script and fails on any mismatch with a message naming the divergent table/index.
The script is also wired as a pre-commit hook so local commits catch drift before push.
executor_brief: Add a schema-drift gate that ensures the committed src/bearings/db/schema.sql matches what the live aiosqlite engine produces from it. Author scripts/check_schema_drift.py: apply schema.sql to memory DB, snapshot sqlite_schema, compare normalised SQL to the source. Wire into ci.yml as a backend-job step and into pre-commit. Failure message must point at the offending object.

verdict_evidence: Today the two strings happen to agree (pyproject.toml `version = "1.0.0"`; docs/openapi.json `info.version = "1.0.0"`), but no CI step asserts the equality. ci.yml backend job (lines 32-82) does not parse either file. A future bump that rolls pyproject.toml without regenerating openapi.json (or vice versa) is silently mergeable. The audit explicitly listed this as a required gate.
acceptance_criteria: A small Python script reads `[project].version` from pyproject.toml (via `tomllib`) and `info.version` from docs/openapi.json (via `json.load`) and exits non-zero with a clear message if they differ.
ci.yml backend job runs the script as a step (cheap, place it before pytest).
Script is also a pre-commit hook so local commits catch the drift.
executor_brief: Add a 10-line version-alignment script and wire it into ci.yml + pre-commit. Read pyproject.toml's [project].version with tomllib and docs/openapi.json's info.version with json.load; assertEqual or sys.exit(1). The script must succeed at HEAD (both are "1.0.0").

audit_text: CI: `api-compat` job using `tufin/oasdiff-action` against previous semver tag.
verdict_evidence: ci.yml has no `api-compat` job and no reference to `tufin/oasdiff-action` (or `oasdiff` in any form). The current jobs are: backend, frontend, e2e, links, pre-commit, systemd-unit. The CHANGELOG §1.0.0 stability commitment promises additive-only evolution within v1.x for paths/schemas (CHANGELOG.md:605-608) — without an oasdiff gate, a breaking change can land in v1.0.x and only be caught at consumer-bug time.
acceptance_criteria: A new ci.yml job `api-compat` runs on push and PR to v1-rebuild.
Job checks out the most recent SemVer tag (`git describe --tags --match 'v*' --abbrev=0`) and the current ref's docs/openapi.json.
Job invokes `tufin/oasdiff-action` (or equivalent oasdiff CLI) with the two specs and fails on any breaking change (per oasdiff's `breaking` mode).
Job is gated so it skips cleanly when no prior SemVer tag exists (e.g. first run after v1.0.0 sets the tag itself); otherwise runs unconditionally.
executor_brief: Add an api-compat job to .github/workflows/ci.yml. Use tufin/oasdiff-action@v1 with `base` set to the docs/openapi.json from the previous SemVer tag (`git fetch --tags && git show <prev-tag>:docs/openapi.json`) and `revision` set to the current commit's docs/openapi.json. Fail-on-breaking. Handle the bootstrap case (no prior tag) by checking the tag list first and exiting 0.

audit_text: Code: pin `operation_id=` on all 133 route decorators across `web/routes/*.py`.
code_paths_checked: src/bearings/web/routes/ (29 .py files, all greppable for operation_id)
verdict_evidence: `grep -c operation_id src/bearings/web/routes/*.py` returns 0 for every file (29 files: approvals.py, checklists.py, checkpoints.py, commands.py, _deps.py, diag.py, fs.py, health.py, history.py, import_db.py, memories.py, messages.py, metrics.py, paired_chats.py, pending.py, preferences.py, quota.py, reorg.py, routing.py, sessions_bulk.py, sessions.py, shell.py, spawn_from_reply.py, tags.py, templates.py, uploads.py, usage.py, vault.py, ws_sessions.py). Without explicit operation_ids, FastAPI auto-generates them from `<function_name>_<path>_<method>_<verb>` — any function rename appears as a breaking change to oasdiff. The audit identifies this as a hard prerequisite for finding 4 (api-compat) to be signal-clean.
acceptance_criteria: Every `@router.<verb>(...)` decorator in src/bearings/web/routes/*.py has an explicit `operation_id="..."` kwarg.

operation_ids are stable, kebab-case-or-snake_case slugs derived from the route's role (e.g. `list-sessions`, `create-session`, `update-tags-sort-order`) — NOT the auto-generated `<fn>_<path>` strings.
operation_ids are unique across the whole app (verifiable by parsing docs/openapi.json after regen and asserting len(set(opIds)) == len(opIds)).
docs/openapi.json is regenerated in the same commit; the operationId values in it match the route decorators.
A new test (e.g. tests/test_operation_ids.py) iterates create_app().openapi()['paths'] and asserts every operation has a non-empty `operationId` and they are all unique.
executor_brief: Pin operation_id on every route in src/bearings/web/routes/*.py — 133 operations across 29 files. Use stable kebab-case slugs (e.g. `list-sessions`, `attach-tag-to-session`). Regenerate docs/openapi.json in the same commit. Add tests/test_operation_ids.py asserting every operation has a unique non-empty operationId. This is the prerequisite that lets feature-13-004's oasdiff gate be signal-clean.

audit_text: Code: add `deprecated=True` to `tag_ids` param and `GET /api/tag-groups` route.
verdict_evidence: Half-fixed: tags.py:170 has `@router.get("/api/tag-groups", response_model=list[str], deprecated=True)` ✓. But sessions.py:255 declares `tag_ids: Annotated[list[int] | None, Query()] = None` — the `Query()` constructor takes no `deprecated=True` kwarg and the docstring at lines 265-267 explicitly calls it 'legacy flat OR (back-compat)' but ships no deprecation flag in OpenAPI. The audit grouped these as a single fix; the param half is missing.
acceptance_criteria: sessions.py:255's `tag_ids` Query gains `deprecated=True` (i.e. `Query(deprecated=True)`).
docs/openapi.json is regenerated; the `tag_ids` parameter on GET /api/sessions has `"deprecated": true` in the parameters array.
Existing `deprecated=True` on GET /api/tag-groups (tags.py:170) is preserved.
No callers in frontend/ or tests/ depend on the absence of the deprecated flag (i.e. nothing fails because the param was renamed — it wasn't).
executor_brief: Quick fix in src/bearings/web/routes/sessions.py:255 — change `tag_ids: Annotated[list[int] | None, Query()] = None` to `tag_ids: Annotated[list[int] | None, Query(deprecated=True)] = None`. Regenerate docs/openapi.json in the same commit. The tag-groups half of the audit's bullet is already fixed at tags.py:170.

audit_text: Code: verify `RoutingDecision` / `RoutingRule` / `SystemRoutingRule` / `QuotaSnapshot` are `@dataclass(frozen=True)`; fix if not.
verdict_evidence: All four classes carry `@dataclass(frozen=True)` at HEAD: agent/routing.py:64 declares it on RoutingDecision (line 65); db/routing.py:65 on RoutingRule (line 66) and db/routing.py:118 on SystemRoutingRule (line 119); agent/quota.py:62 on QuotaSnapshot (line 63). The audit's bullet was 'verify ... fix if not' — the verify side is satisfied at HEAD. No resolving commit identifiable because the audit didn't claim it was broken; it asked for confirmation, which we now have.
executor_brief: No action required. Verifier confirmed all four dataclasses are frozen at HEAD.

audit_text: Docs: behavior docs for all `[Unreleased]` features before v1.1.0 tag.
code_paths_checked: docs/behavior/ (14 .md files: bearings-cli, chat, checklists, context-menus, keyboard-shortcuts, memories, modals, paired-chats, preferences, prompt-endpoint, sessions, themes, tool-output-streaming, vault)
verdict_evidence: [Unreleased] adds five surfaces the audit named: tag classes, import-from-bearings, SDK history replay, preferences, permission-mode. docs/behavior/ has dedicated docs only for `preferences.md`. No `tag-classes.md`, no `import-bearings.md` (or import.md), no `sdk-history.md` (or sdk-session-replay.md), no `permission-mode.md`. `tag class` is mentioned in chat.md and context-menus.md but not as a contractual behavior doc; `sdk_session` shows up in sessions.md but only as an aside; `permission_mode` appears in chat.md and preferences.md only in passing. The audit's threshold is explicit: 'must be resolved before v1.1.0 ships or contract is violated on day 1 of second release.'
acceptance_criteria: docs/behavior/tag-classes.md exists and documents: the `class` column values (project/severity/general), per-class cardinality at the API boundary, severity-class validator rejecting default_model/working_dir, `sort_order` semantics + PUT /api/tags/sort-order, the three `tag_ids_<class>` query params on GET /api/sessions (OR within, AND across), and `severity_none=true` semantics.

docs/behavior/import-bearings.md exists and documents: POST /api/import/bearings flow, source path resolution, transactional semantics (all-or-nothing), duplicate-skip rule by primary key, column mappings for schema differences, ImportResult shape returned to /settings UI.

docs/behavior/sdk-history-replay.md exists and documents: SessionStore adapter contract, sdk_session_entries mirror table role, deterministic UUID derivation `ses_<32hex> → <8>-<4>-<4>-<4>-<12>`, first-spawn vs subsequent-spawn kwargs (`session_id=` vs `resume=`), mutual-exclusion guard, the four respawn triggers (model swap, idle reap, server restart, ERROR recovery).

docs/behavior/permission-mode.md exists and documents: KNOWN_SDK_PERMISSION_MODES, update_permission_mode DB helper semantics, None = clear-and-use-profile-default, the toggle's UI surface and persistence point, the runner's read point on next boot.
All four docs follow the existing docs/behavior/<subsystem>.md style (heading hierarchy, code-shape boundaries, behavior-not-implementation framing) — match e.g. preferences.md or paired-chats.md.
lychee passes on all four new files.
executor_brief: Author four new docs/behavior/*.md files for the contract-bearing [Unreleased] surfaces named in the audit: tag-classes, import-bearings, sdk-history-replay, permission-mode. Match the style of preferences.md / paired-chats.md (observable-behavior contracts, not implementation walkthroughs). Each doc is self-contained and ≥ 60 lines. Source the spec from the [Unreleased] CHANGELOG entries and the cited code paths.

audit_text: Docs: add paragraph to CHANGELOG v1.0.0 stability section scoping WS message shapes and CLI flag surface (in or explicitly out).
verdict_evidence: CHANGELOG.md:601-626 enumerates four scoped surfaces: HTTP API (paths/schemas), Routing/quota wire shapes, On-disk schema, Behavior docs. Lines 623-626 list the things that are explicitly NOT changing (localhost posture, single-user model, SQLite backend, orphan history) but say nothing about WebSocket message shapes (`/ws/sessions/{id}` traffic — see docs/behavior/tool-output-streaming.md) or the `bearings serve|init|gc|todo|migrate` CLI flag surface. The audit asks these be explicitly scoped 'in or explicitly out.'
acceptance_criteria: CHANGELOG.md §1.0.0 'Stability commitment' gains an explicit clause naming WebSocket message shapes (the JSON payloads on `/ws/sessions/{id}` and any other ws routes) as either part of the SemVer-governed surface or explicitly out-of-scope.

Same section gains an explicit clause naming the bearings CLI flag surface (`bearings serve --host/--port`, `bearings init`, `bearings gc`, `bearings todo`, `bearings migrate`) as either in-scope or explicitly out-of-scope.

If 'in', the clause names where the contract lives (e.g. docs/behavior/tool-output-streaming.md for ws, src/bearings/cli/ for CLI). If 'out', the clause states the rationale (e.g. 'WS framing remains evolution-friendly within v1.x' or 'CLI flags follow Typer convention and may rename in v1.x').
The new clauses follow the bullet style already used in lines 605-621.
executor_brief: Edit CHANGELOG.md §1.0.0 'Stability commitment' (lines 601-626): add two bullets — one for WebSocket message shapes, one for the CLI flag surface — each explicitly placing the surface IN or OUT of the SemVer envelope. Match the bullet style of the existing four (HTTP API / Routing-quota / On-disk schema / Behavior docs). Cite the contract location for in-scope surfaces; cite the rationale for out-of-scope ones.

audit_text: Docs: establish deprecation convention — `deprecated=True` + `x-sunset` extension + `Sunset` response header from middleware.
verdict_evidence: `grep -rn 'x-sunset\|Sunset' src/bearings/ docs/` returns no results. There is no middleware that emits a `Sunset` response header on deprecated routes, no `x-sunset` OpenAPI extension on any deprecated decorator, and no docs/ page that documents the deprecation convention. The single existing deprecated route (GET /api/tag-groups, tags.py:170) carries only `deprecated=True` — half of the convention the audit prescribes.
acceptance_criteria: A new docs/deprecation-convention.md (or a section under docs/architecture-v1.md) documents the three-part convention: route/param `deprecated=True` + OpenAPI `x-sunset: <ISO-8601 date>` extension + runtime `Sunset` response header.

A new ASGI middleware (e.g. src/bearings/web/middleware/sunset.py) emits a `Sunset: <ISO-8601 date>` response header for any request whose matched route has been registered as deprecated with a sunset date.
GET /api/tag-groups (tags.py:170) and the `tag_ids` Query (sessions.py:255 — see finding 6) gain an `x-sunset` extension naming the v1.x release in which they will be removed (e.g. v1.2.0).
docs/openapi.json is regenerated; the `x-sunset` extensions are visible on the deprecated path/parameter.
An e2e or unit test asserts the `Sunset` header is present on a request to GET /api/tag-groups and absent on a non-deprecated route.
executor_brief: Establish the deprecation convention end-to-end. (1) Author docs/deprecation-convention.md describing the deprecated=True + x-sunset + Sunset-header trio. (2) Add x-sunset to the existing deprecated surfaces (GET /api/tag-groups; tag_ids param after finding 6 lands). (3) Author src/bearings/web/middleware/sunset.py: ASGI middleware that, on each response, looks up the matched route and emits `Sunset: <date>` if the route's openapi_extra carries x-sunset. (4) Wire it in create_app(). (5) Regenerate docs/openapi.json. (6) Add a small unit/integration test.

audit_text: Docs/session: update stale "62 paths / 53 schemas" (v0.18.0 baseline) → current **106 paths / 94 schemas / 133 operations**.
code_paths_checked: CHANGELOG.md:593-594 (v1.0.0 'public HTTP surface (62 paths, 53 schemas)' — stale)
CHANGELOG.md:780-781 (v0.18.0 'OpenAPI export ... 62 paths, 53 schemas' — accurate as v0.18.0 baseline)
docs/openapi.json (live: 106 paths, 94 schemas, 133 operations confirmed by python -c json.load count)
verdict_evidence: CHANGELOG.md:593-594 says 'The public HTTP surface (62 paths, 53 schemas — see docs/openapi.json)' inside the v1.0.0 release entry. The live docs/openapi.json at HEAD reports 106 paths / 94 schemas / 133 operations (verified: `python -c "import json; spec=json.load(open('docs/openapi.json')); paths=spec['paths']; ops=sum(1 for p in paths.values() for m in p if m in ('get','post','put','patch','delete','options','head','trace')); print(len(paths), len(spec.get('components',{}).get('schemas',{})), ops)"` → 106 94 133). The v0.18.0 entry on lines 780-781 keeps 62/53 as a historical baseline — leave that alone. Only the v1.0.0 sentence is stale.
acceptance_criteria: CHANGELOG.md:593-594 reads '106 paths, 94 schemas, 133 operations' (or equivalent phrasing) instead of '62 paths, 53 schemas'.
CHANGELOG.md:780-781 (v0.18.0 entry) is preserved as-is — that count is historically correct for v0.18.0.
If a regen of docs/openapi.json in some other PR shifts the counts, this PR's number is the count at the resolving-commit's docs/openapi.json (re-derive at commit time).
executor_brief: One-line fix: update CHANGELOG.md:593-594 from '62 paths, 53 schemas' to '106 paths, 94 schemas, 133 operations' (counts at HEAD's docs/openapi.json). Leave CHANGELOG.md:780-781 (v0.18.0 entry) untouched — its 62/53 figure is the v0.18.0 baseline, not stale. Re-derive the counts at commit time if other in-flight PRs change docs/openapi.json.
